texto: We may preach with all the fervor of a reformer and yet succeed only in winning a straggler here and there, if we permit the whole collective thought of the nation or of the world to be controlled by ideas which, by the resistless force of logic, prevent Christianity from being regarded as anything more than a harmless delusion...What is to-day a matter of academic speculation, begins to-morrow to move armies and pull down empires.
explicacao: Citação de J. Gresham Machen sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “We may preach with all the fervor of a reformer and yet succeed only in winning a straggler here …” articula sucesso, christian-intellect, ideas em registro de sucesso, com relevância para contextos como ideas e theology.

frase_original: We may preach with all the fervor of a reformer and yet succeed only in winning a straggler here and there, if we permit the whole collective thought of the nation or of the world to be controlled by ideas which, by the resistless force of logic, prevent Christianity from being regarded as anything more than a harmless delusion...What is to-day a matter of academic speculation, begins to-morrow to move armies and pull down empires.

texto: What I need first of all is not exhortation, but a gospel, not directions for saving myself but knowledge of how God has saved me. Have you any good news? That is the question that I ask of you. I know your exhortations will not help me. But if anything has been done to save me, will you not tell me the facts?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de J. Gresham Machen que provoca temas ligados a sabedoria. Ao questionar “What I need first of all is not exhortation, but a gospel, not directions for saving myself but k…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre sabedoria, christ, good-news, com foco em good-news e gospel.
